message:
Add custom comparators for sort function (#5767)

## Summary

- Extends the `sort()` function to accept an optional `cmp` parameter
for custom comparators
- Enables sorting lists by complex criteria using binary lambda
expressions
- Implements validation to ensure `cmp` takes a binary lambda (two
parameters)
- Restricts binary lambdas to `sort(..., cmp=...)` context only
- Adds comprehensive test cases for ascending/descending order and error
cases

## Details

The sort function now supports custom comparison logic through a binary
lambda that receives two adjacent elements and returns a boolean
indicating the sort order. This allows flexible sorting of lists of
records.

Example usage:
```
sort(xs, cmp=(left, right) => left.value < right.value)
```

The implementation includes:
- Enhanced `lambda_expr` AST to support both unary and binary lambdas
- Updated parser to handle lambda parameters as a vector
- New evaluation logic for sort predicates with warning state tracking
- Updated aggregate functions (`count_if`, `where`, `map`) to enforce
unary lambdas only
- Test cases covering binary comparators with ascending/descending order

## Documentation PR

- https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/218

## Test plan

- Run integration tests: `uvx tenzir-test --root test --match "*sort*"`
- Verify new test cases for custom comparators
- Verify error handling for non-binary lambdas used elsewhere

<sub>Resolves TNZ-322</sub>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+title: Enhance `sort` function with `desc` and `cmp` parameters
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - codex
+pr: 5767
+created: 2026-02-17T06:37:14.758079Z
+---
+
+The `sort` function now supports two new parameters: `desc` for controlling
+sort direction and `cmp` for custom comparison logic via binary lambdas.
+
+**Sort in descending order:**
+
+```tql
+from {xs: [3, 1, 2]}
+select ys = sort(xs, desc=true)
+```
+
+```tql
+{ys: [3, 2, 1]}
+```
+
+**Sort records by a specific field using a custom comparator:**
+
+```tql
+from {xs: [{v: 2, id: "b"}, {v: 1, id: "a"}, {v: 2, id: "c"}]}
+select ys = sort(xs, cmp=(left, right) => left.v < right.v)
+```
+
+```tql
+{
+  ys: [
+    {v: 1, id: "a"},
+    {v: 2, id: "b"},
+    {v: 2, id: "c"},
+  ],
+}
+```
+
+The `cmp` lambda receives two elements and returns a boolean indicating whether
+the first element should come before the second. Both parameters can be combined
+to reverse a custom comparison.

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+---
+error: true
+---
+
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+select ys = xs.where((left, right) => left > right)

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+error: expected unary lambda for `where`
+ --> tests/functions/sort/binary_lambda_rejected_elsewhere.tql:6:23
+  |
+6 | select ys = xs.where((left, right) => left > right)
+  |                       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
+  |
+  = hint: binary lambdas are only supported for `sort(..., cmp=...)`

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+---
+error: true
+---
+
+from {xs: [3, 1, 2]}
+select ys = sort(xs, cmp=x => x < x)

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+error: `cmp` must be a binary lambda
+ --> tests/functions/sort/cmp_requires_binary_lambda.tql:6:26
+  |
+6 | select ys = sort(xs, cmp=x => x < x)
+  |                          ^^^^^^^^^^ 
+  |
+  = hint: provide `cmp=(a, b) => ...`

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+---
+fixtures: [http]
+error: true
+---
+
+from {url: env("HTTP_FIXTURE_LINK_URL")}
+http url, paginate=((left, right) => left) { read_json }

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+error: expected unary lambda
+ --> tests/operators/from_http/http_paginate_binary_lambda_rejected.tql:7:22
+  |
+7 | http url, paginate=((left, right) => left) { read_json }
+  |                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
+  |
+  = hint: binary lambdas are only supported for `sort(..., cmp=...)`
